abstract: Display five images as mosaic tiles.
overview:
## Overview

A mosaic lets you display images as part of a larger collection.

**On this page, you’ll learn how to use a mosaic to display images as a set of tiles.**



### Add a Mosaic with Captions in Your Article

In [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/setting-up-the-introductory-tutorial#Download-the-Article-Bundle-Examples], you downloaded a bundle called `News_Design_Tutorial_5_Galleries` that contains gallery images and mosaic images. Now, you’ll move some of those images into your working folder and create a `mosaic` component to display the images.

In [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/positioning-text-components] and [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/adding-an-image-and-captions], you created some `ComponentLayout` objects. You’ll refer to those `ComponentLayout` objects in this code.

Your code should look like the example code [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/adding-a-mosaic-of-images#Mosaic-Result].

After you make this change in your code, you can preview your working `article.json` file in News Preview to see a mosaic in your article.

#### Mosaic: Copy This Code

#### Mosaic: Result

Ellipses (`...`) indicate lines of code that have been omitted from this example.

### Further Exploration

At any time, you can try changing property values in your `article.json` file and then use News Preview to see how these changes affect the look of your article.

For example, try changing the order of images in your `mosaic` component by changing URLs:

The order of your first and second images has been switched.

### Previous

[doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/adding-a-gallery-of-images]

### Next

[doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/adding-a-tweet]
